rulings.Case Title: SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FISHER DEVELOPMENT INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs; Meorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of John F. Rutan Jr
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs. Plaintiff Scottsdale Insurance Company's motion to tax costs is denied. The memorandum of costs filed by defendants was timely filed since it is undisputed that notice of entry of judgment or dismissal was never served. Defendants did not act in any way that would constitute a waiver of their right to seek costs. Defendants are prevailing parties for purposes of the costs statutes since all claims against them were dismissed. All costs sought to be taxed were reasonably incurred and reasonably necessary and are allowed per the court's discretion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LISA BROWN VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Service Of The Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Christopher J. Cummiskey Esq., In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT TEWELDE GEBREMARIAM'S Motion To Quash Service Of The Complaint. Special Appearing Defendant Tewelde Gebremariam's motion to quash service of the summons and complaint on defendant Gebremariam is granted. By opposing the motion on its merits, plaintiff Lisa Brown waived her objection based on untimely service of the moving papers. (Reedy v. Bussell (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 1272, 1288). Ms. Brown failed to provide sufficient evidence to satisfy her burden to prove that service of summons and complaint made on Mr. Gebremariam was proper. The declaration of Ms. Barhe shows that she resides at the purported service address, she did not accept service for Mr. Gebremariam, Mr. Gebremariam has been in Ethiopia since September 2016, and was in Ethiopia on February 2018 when he was allegedly personally served. The proof of service dated February 9, 2018 lacks any physical description of the man that was personally served. Mr. Hutson's declaration is insufficient evidence because he states that he personally served Mr. Gebremariam or a "person authorized to receive service of process" for Mr. Gebremariam and offered no explanation as to the person "who claimed to be" Mr. Gebremariam. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Gebremariam is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA COHEN VS. UNUM GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 2, Defendant Unum Group's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Tony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As to Interrogatory Nos, 6 and 7, the motion is granted. As to Interrogatory No. 12, the motion is granted. As to Interrogatory No. 14, the motion is granted. As to Interrogatory No. 16, the motion is granted-in-part and denied-in-part. Plaintiff must respond with insurance policies going back to the beginning of 2011. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA COHEN VS. UNUM GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 2, Defendant Unum Group's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Tony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As to Requests Nos. 7, 8 and 23, the motion is granted. By limiting her response to documents that she "believes" are "reasonably responsive" to the requests, Plaintiff has rendered illusory her agreement to produce responsive documents. Plaintiff must provide a Code-compliant response to these requests. As to Requests No. 10, granted as to documents going back to the beginning of 2011. As to Request No. 12, granted as to documents going back to the beginning of 2011. As to Requests Nos. 16 and 33-36, the parties should be prepared to discuss these at the hearing, including to update the Court on the status of the in-camera review conducted pursuant to the Court's March 20, 2018 order regarding Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoena served on the City of Oakland. As to Request No. 18, granted-in-part and denied-in-part. Plaintiff shall respond only with respect to prescriptions related to fibromyalgia and associated back and next pain (i.e., the medical conditions(s) for which she claims total disability) going back to the beginning of 2011. As to Requests Nos. 19-22, granted-in-part and denied-in-part. Plaintiff shall respond with documents sufficient to show the identities of her co-workers, supervisors, etc. covered by these requests. As to Requests Nos. 24-26, granted-in-part and denied-in-part. Plaintiff shall respond with documents sufficient to show travel to destinations greater than 100 miles from point of origin during the requested time period. As to Requests Nos. 27, 29 and 30, the motion is denied. These requests seek private financial information , and the requests are broadly drawn to capture information that is not directly relevant to the issues in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ ET AL VS. LEANA PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF BIANA CHERNOGUZ, VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ'S Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Plaintiffs Biana and Vladislav Chernoguz's motion for leave to amend complaint is denied. The new claims sought to be alleged by the plaintiffs are already encompassed by the Rent Board case, which is the subject of CPF-16-515041, a case pending in this court. Granting the relief sought by plaintiffs not only contravenes the November 3, 2017 demurrer order by Judge Quidachay in this case, but violates well-established principles of alleging claims involving the same primary right in two different lawsuits. The court expresses no view whether the claims sought to be alleged by plaintiffs could be permissibly alleged in case 515041. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VERONICA MCCLUSKEY VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC'S. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant Airbnb, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings regarding the claims alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Veronica McCluskey. Airbnb has shown that Ms. McCluskey assented to the Terms of Service, including the arbitration language therein, because the sign up screen adequately informed Ms. McCluskey that, by clicking to sign up, she was agreeing to the Terms of Service. Under both the Federal Arbitration Act and the California Arbitration Act parties may delegate issues of arbitrability to the arbitrator, including disputes regarding the scope and the enforceability of the arbitration agreement and whether the arbitration agreement is unconscionable. (Aanderdund v, Superior Court (2017) 13 Cal. App. 5th 880, 892; Tiri v. Lucky Chances, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 231, 240). The arbitration language of the Terms and Services is clear and unmistakable evidence of the parties' intent to delegate arbitrability. Unlike Baker v. Osborne Development Corp. (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 884, 891, there is no provision in the Terms of Service that refers to both the court and the arbitrator as having jurisdiction to rule on the enforceability of clauses of the agreement. The reference to state and federal courts in California in the section entitled "Controlling Law and Jurisdiction" pertains to the carve-out for injunctive and equitable relief and, per Aanderud, 13 Cal.App.5th at 893-894, this does create any ambiguity as to whether arbitrability has been delegated to the arbitrator. Ms. McCluskey does not appear to contend that the delegation clause is unconscionable separate and apart from the remainder of the arbitration agreement, which would be an issue to be decided by the court rather than the arbitrator. But even if she did, she does not identify anything substantively unconscionable about the delegation clause, so that clause is valid and enforceable per the unconscionability doctrine. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Airbnb is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
